macro_segments.title: Digression on Drunkenness, Propriety Before God, and Fitness for Prayer and Judgment

micro_segments.title: Reviewing Previous Discussion: Non-Jew's Ownership and Hired Hands as Partners in Eruv
Rav Nachman's Comments on Psak, Drunkenness, and the Problem of Giving Rulings While Drunk
Liability of Drunken Person: Business Transactions Binding Unless Drunk as Lot; Exemption from Prayer Due to Loss of Kavana
Rabbi Chanina: Passing Over Magein Avraham When Drunk—Definition of Mafik (Pass Over vs. Reveal)
Gemara's Linguistic Analysis: Afik as Pass-Over vs. Afik as Reveal; Connection to Pasuk Sources
Difference Between Two Interpretations: Proactive Waiting vs. Passive Restraint (Rav Sheshet's Sleep)
Rabbeinu Chananel's Alternative: Ga'ava (Pride/Yetzer Hara) as the Real Issue, Not Drunkenness
Transition: From Drunkenness to Distractedness—Rav Chiya bar Ashi on Unsettled Mind (Da'as Meyushevet)
Applying Requirement to Rav: Clarity Needed for Halacha (Hilchasa Ba'ya Tzilusa)
Personal Distractibility Stories: Abaye's Mother, Rava/Ravina, and the Louse Bite
Night vs. Day Learning: Disagreement Between Rav Huna (Night for Sleep) and Reish Lakish (Moon for Learning)
Practical Examples: Rabbi Zeira's Sharp Learning (Daytime Focus), Rav Chisda and Daughter (Sleep Discussion)
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: 'We Are Day Workers' and Rav Acha bar Yaakov's Borrowing Against Daytime

macro_segments.title: Conditions for Valid Prayer: Travel Recovery, Environmental Distractions, and Emotional States

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar: Three-Day Recovery After Journey Before Prayer (Pasuk Source)
Personal Practices: Shmuel and Rav Pappa—Avoidance of Houses with Drinking/Fish Frying

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Passage on Wine: Its Purposes, Character Revelation, and Blessing

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chanina: Those Appeased by Wine Possess Divine Quality (Pasuk on God's Pleasure in Sacrifice)

macro_segments.title: Conditions for Valid Prayer: Travel Recovery, Environmental Distractions, and Emotional States (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rishonic Debate: Application of These Exemptions in Modern Times (Lowered Threshold of Kavana)

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Passage on Wine: Its Purposes, Character Revelation, and Blessing (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya: One Who Holds Wine Shows Wisdom of Seventy Elders (Gematria: Yayin = Sod = 70)
Rabbi Chanina: Wine Created to Console Mourners and Reward Wicked (Pasuk from Kohelet)
Rabbi Chanin bar Pappa: Wine as Plentiful as Water = Blessing (Ma'aser Sheni Connection)
Rabbi Ilai: Three Things Reveal Character—Drunkenness, Business, Anger (Plus Joking per Some)

macro_segments.title: Eruv in Two Connected Courtyards: Jew + Non-Jew Configuration and Rabbi Akiva's Rule

micro_segments.title: Setup: Inner Courtyard (Jew + Non-Jew) vs. Outer (Jew); Which Configuration Is Forbidden?
Rav's Position: Inner Courtyard Alone Is Permitted; Problem Only in Outer Due to All Three Together
Gemara Analysis: Not Rabbi Meir (One Jew = Problem) but Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov + Rabbi Akiva
Rabbi Akiva's Rule: Permitted Inner Courtyard Makes Troubles for Outer; Outer Must Include Inner in Eruv
Reversed Scenario: Outer (Jew + Non-Jew) vs. Inner (Jew Only)—Same Halakha Based on Common Cases

macro_segments.title: Practical Eruv Case: Renting from Landlord in Multi-Room Inn When Tenant Is Absent

micro_segments.title: Scenario: Reish Lakish and Students at Inn; One Room Rented by Non-Jew, Tenant Absent
Question: Can We Rent from Landlord When Current Tenant (Who Has Rights) Is Absent?
Condition for Renting: Landlord's Ability to Evict/Reassign the Tenant (De-rabbanan Ruling)
Practical Resolution: Reish Lakish Decides to Rent from Landlord and Later Ask Rav Afes (Southern Masters)
Rav Afes's Validation: 'You Did Well' to Rent from Landlord (Modern Eruv Implications)

Daytime learning vs. nighttime: Rav Huna (sleep) vs. Reish Lakish (moon)
Travel recovery — three days before prayer (Eiruv Yam narrative)
Environmental distractions in prayer — Shmuel (drinking house), Rav Pappa (fish fry)
Rishonic debate: Yeridas HaDoros — lowered threshold of kavana in modern times
Yayin (wine) gematria = 70; Sod (secret) gematria = 70; wine reveals secrets

Rabbi Meir — one Jew creates eruv problem
Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov — two Jews create problem; one Jew does not
